title: Water reclamation using graphene oxide films
abstract: Systems, devices, and methods are described herein for generating liquid water from water vapor present in the atmosphere through use of selectively permeable films that permit water to pass through but that block other liquids and gases, such as atmospheric gases. The systems, devices, and methods employ condensation techniques in which only the water that is passed through the selectively permeable film is cooled, as compared to other atmospheric water generation systems, which cool water and other gases that enter with the water.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: . A system for atmospheric water reclamation, comprising: an enclosure defining an internal volume, wherein the enclosure prevents gases from permeating into or out of the internal volume, wherein at least a portion of the enclosure is a liquid water permeable region that allows water molecules to permeate into or out of the internal volume, and wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises: an interlocking layer structure including a system of nano-capillaries, wherein liquid water is permitted to pass from a first surface of the interlocking layer structure to a second surface of the interlocking layer structure through the system of nano-capillaries, and wherein gases are incapable of passing through the system of nano-capillaries; a heat transfer surface positioned within the internal volume, wherein the heat transfer surface is configured to remove heat from within the internal volume such that water vapor present within the internal volume condenses on the heat transfer surface to form condensed liquid water; and a reservoir positioned within the internal volume to collect the condensed liquid water. 2, The system of claim 1, wherein the liquid water permeable region allows water molecules to permeate into the internal volume by water molecules, in vapor form present in an atmosphere external to the enclosure, condensing on the first surface of the interlocking layer structure and changing to liquid form, passing as a liquid through the system of nano-capillaries, and evaporating from the second surface of the interlocking layer structure to increase a partial pressure of water within the internal volume. 3, The system of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises a graphene oxide film. 4. The system of any of claims 1-3, wherein the interlocking layer structure comprises a graphene oxide laminate including a plurality of graphene oxide crystallites. 5. The system of any of claims 1-4, wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises a thin film having a thickness between 0.1 Î¼Î·Î¹ and 5 Î¼ÏÎ·. 6. The system of any of claims 1-5, wherein nano-capillaries of the system of nano-capillaries have a dimension selected between 5 A and 15 A. 7. The system of any of claims 1-6, wherein the liquid water permeable region is formed by steps including: dispersing graphite oxide in water by soni cation to form a stable suspension of graphene oxide crystallites; spray-coating or spin-coating the stable suspension of graphene oxide crystallites over a supporting film; and removing at least a portion of the supporting film, thereby generating the liquid water permeable region. 8. The system of any of claims 1-7, wherein the liquid water permeable region exhibits a gas permeation rate less than or equal to 10 "8 mnrg / cm 2 - s-bar. 9. The system of any of claims 1-8, wherein the liquid water permeable region exhibits a water permeation rate greater than or equal to 10 '5 mnrg / cm 2 -s-bar. 10. The system of any of claims 1-9, wherein the heat transfer surface comprises a condensing surface that has a temperature below ambient temperature. 11. The system of any of claims 1-10, wherein the heat transfer surface comprises an internal surface of the enclosure. 12. The system of any of claims 1-1 1, wherein the heat transfer surface comprises a surface of a heat exchanger that is positioned in thermal communication with a heat pump or refrigeration system. 13. The system of any of claims 1-12, wherein the enclosure comprises one or more materials selected from the group consisting of plastic, metal, glass, polymer, Teflon, Kapton, and any combination of these. 14. The system of any of claims 1-13, wherein the enclosure exhibits a gas permeation rate less than or equal to 1() "8 mnrg / cm 2 - s-bar. 15. The system of any of claims 1-14, further comprising a fan or blower for directing air from an atmosphere external to the enclosure towards the liquid water permeable region. 16. The system of any of claims 1-15, further comprising a fan or blower within the internal volume for circulating gases within the internal volume over an internal surface of the liquid water permeable region. 17. The system of any of claims 1-16, further comprising a fan or blower within the internal volume for circulating gases within the internal volume over the heat tra sfer surface, 8. The system of any of claims 1-17, further comprising a pump within the internal volume for pumping liquid water out of the reservoir. 19. The system of any of claims 1-18, further comprising a support structure attached to the enclosure for supporting the liquid water permeable region. 20. The system of any of claim s 1-19, wherein the enclosure comprises a balloon, wherein the heat transfer surface corresponds to an inner surface of the balloon, and wherein the water reservoir corresponds to a bottom portion of the balloon. 21. The system of claim 20, wherein the enclosure comprises a first balloon including a first lifting gas within the internal volume, the system further comprising: a second balloon comprising: a second enclosure defining a second internal volume including a second lifting gas, wherein the second enclosure prevents gas from permeating into or out of the second internal volume, wherein at least a portion of the second enclosure is a second liquid water permeable region that allows water molecules to permeate into or out of the second internal volume; a second heat transfer surface positioned within the second internal volume, wherein the second heat transfer surface is configured to remove heat from within the second internal volume such that water vapor present within the second internal volume condenses on the second heat transfer surface to form condensed liquid water; and 

 a second reservoir positioned within the second internal volume to collect condensed liquid water; a pulley system configured for raising the first balloon while lowering the second balloon and for lowering the first balloon while raising the second balloon, wherein the first balloon and the second balloon are each attached to the pulley system; and a motor attached to the pulley system for raising and lowering the first balloon and the second balloon. 22. An atmospheric water reclamation device, comprising: a membrane, wherein at least a portion of the membrane is a liquid water permeable region, wherein the membrane prevents gases from permeating into or out of an internal volume surrounded by the membrane, wherein the liquid water permeable region allow water molecules to permeate into or out of the internal volume, wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises: an interlocking layer structure including a system of nano-capillaries, wherein liquid water is permitted to pass from a first surface of the interlocking layer structure to a second surface of the interlocking layer structure through the system of nano-capillaries, and wherein gases are incapable of passing through the system of nano-capillaries. 23. The atmospheric water reclamation device of claim 22, further comprising a lifting gas trapped within the membrane. 24. The atmospheric water reclamation device of claim 22 or claim 23, wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises a graphene oxide film. 25. The atmospheric water reclamation device of any of claims 22-24, wherein the interlocking layer structure comprises a graphene oxide laminate including a plurality of graphene oxide crystallites. 26. The atmospheric water reclamation device of any of claims 22-25, wherein the membrane comprises one or more materials selected from the group consisting of Mylar, latex, poly chloroprene, nylon, and any combination of these. 27. The atmospheric water reclamation device of any of claims 22-26, wherein an internal surface of the membrane is a heat transfer surface for removing heat from 

 within the internal volume such that water vapor present in gases within the internal volume condenses on the internal surface to form condensed liquid water. 28. The atmospheric water reclamation device of any of claim s 22-27, wherein the liquid water permeable region is formed by steps including: dispersing graphite oxide in water by sonication to form a stable suspension of graph en e oxide crystallites; spray-coating or spin-coating the stable suspension of graphene oxide crystallites over a supporting film; and removing at least a portion of the supporting film, thereby generating the li qui d water perm eable region . 29. The atmospheric water reclamation device of any of claims 22-28, further comprising a structural film positioned adjacent to all or a portion of the liquid water permeable region. 30. The atmospheric water reclamation device of claim 29, wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises a coating positioned adjacent to the stmctural film. 31. The atmospheric water reclamation device of claim 29 or claim 30, wherein the structural film is permeable to water. 32. The atmospheric water reclamation device of any of claims 29-31, wherein the structural film is permeable to gases. 33. A method for atmospheric water reclamation, comprising: providing an enclosure surrounding an internal volume, wherein the enclosure prevents gases from permeating into or out of the internal volume, wherein at least a portion of the enclosure is a liquid water permeable region that allows water molecules to permeate into or out of the internal volume, and wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises: an interlocking layer structure including a system of nano-capillaries, wherein liquid water is permitted to pass from a first surface of the interlocking layer structure to a second surface of the interlocking layer structure through the sy stem of nano-capillaries, and wherein gases are incapable of passing through the system of nano-capillaries, 

 cooling a heat transfer surface positioned within the internal volume to remove heat from gases within the internal volume such that water vapor present in the gases within the internal volume condenses on the heat transfer surface to form condensed liquid water; and collecting the condensed liquid water in a reservoir within the internal volume. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the enclosure comprises a balloon filled with a lifting gas, wherein the heat transfer surface comprises an internal surface of the balloon, and wherein cooling the heat transfer surface includes increasing an elevation of the balloon from a first altitude having a first atmospheric temperature to a second altitude having a second atmospheric temperature that is lower than the first atmospheric temperature. 35. The method of claim 33 or claim 34, wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises a graphene oxide film. 36. The method of any of claims 33-35, wherein the interlocking layer structure compri ses a graphene oxide laminate including a plurality of graphene oxide crystallites. 37. The method of any of claims 33-36, wherein the liquid water permeable region is formed by steps including: dispersing graphite oxide in water by soni cation to form a stable suspension of graphene oxide crystallites; spray-coating or spin-coating the stable suspension of graphene oxide crystallites over a supporting film; and removing at least a portion of the supporting film, thereby generating the liquid water permeable region. 38. A method for making an apparatus for atmospheric water reclamation, comprising: providing an enclosure for surrounding an internal volume; and creating a liquid water permeable region within a wall of the enclosure, wherein the liquid water permeable region allows water molecules to permeate into or out of the internal volume, wherein the enclosure and the liquid water permeable region together prevents gas molecules and gas atoms from permeating into or out of the internal volume, and wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises: 

 an interlocking layer structure including a system of nano-capillaries, wherein liquid water is permitted to pass from a first surface of the interlocking layer structure to a second surface of the interlocking layer structure through the system of nano-capillaries, and wherein gases are incapable of passing through the system of nano-capillaries. 39. The method of claim 38, further comprising: providing a heat transfer surface within the internal volume, wherein the heat transfer surface is configured to remove heat from within the internal volume such that water vapor present in gases within the internal volume condenses on the heat transfer surface to form condensed liquid water. 40. The method of claim 39, further compri sing: providing a reservoir within the internal volume for collecting the condensed liquid water. 41. The method of any of claims 38-40, wherein the enclosure includes a supporting structure for supporting the liquid water permeable region. 42. The method of any of claims 38-41, wherein the liquid water permeable region comprises a graphene oxide film. 43. The method of any of claims 38-42, wherein the interlocking layer structure comprises a graphene oxide laminate including a plurality of graphene oxide crystallites. 44. The method of any of claims 38-43, wherein creating the liquid water permeable region includes forming a graphene oxide film by: dispersing graphite oxide in water by sonication to form a stable suspension of graphene oxide crystallites; spray-coating or spin-coating the stable suspension of graphene oxide crystallites over a supporting film; and removing at least a portion of the supporting film. 45, The method of any of claims 38-44, wherein the apparatus comprises a stem of any of claims 1-21 or a device of any of claims 22Â·
